summary:
Design a data structure that tracks points on a 2D plane and can count axis-aligned squares formed with a given query point.

Implement a class DetectSquares with three behaviors: the constructor initializes an empty structure; add(point) records a point [x, y] (the same point may be added more than once); and count(point) returns how many axis-aligned squares can be formed using the query point [x, y] together with three other points already stored. The four corners of such a square must all be present, and the square's sides must be parallel to the axes with non-zero area. Points added multiple times are counted with their multiplicity.

examples.explanation: After adding [3,10], [11,2], and [3,2], count([11,10]) finds exactly one square with corners [3,10],[11,2],[3,2],[11,10]. count([14,8]) finds 0. After adding [11,2] a second time, count([11,10]) returns 2 because the duplicated point doubles the square count.

constraints: At most 3000 calls in total are made to add and count
Only axis-aligned squares with positive area are counted
